message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save(). I'll touch Image.py save() method and its tests. Please propose your files so we keep edits disjoint; if you also need Image.py let's coordinate on the specific region (save method vs elsewhere).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding dry_run param to Image.save() in Image.py (near line 2469), plus tests in Tests/test_image.py. My changes are localized to the save() method body. Let me know if your feature also touches Image.py so we can split non-overlapping regions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Our features both touch Image.save() and test_image.py, so full file-disjointness isn't possible. Accepting overlap: I'll implement backup/backup_suffix params, you implement dry_run. To minimize merge conflicts: I'll add my backup=False, backup_suffix='.bak' params at the END of save()'s kwarg list (after **params, i.e. as explicit keyword args before **params) and add my logic as a self-contained block right at the top of save() body (checking backup before opening fp). Please add your dry_run param/logic in a distinct block so our diffs touch different lines. We'll each work on our own branch and resolve the merge conflict on the signature/docstring lines manually at merge time. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Both features touch Image.save() in the same files - that's unavoidable given our features. Accepting overlap; I'll keep my dry_run change small and localized: (1) pop 'dry_run' from params near where 'save_all' is popped (~line 2546), (2) in the fp-open block (~lines 2561-2591) skip real file open/create/delete/close when dry_run is True (write to an io.BytesIO instead) so save_handler still runs. I won't touch anything else in the method. We'll each commit to our own branch and I'll run the required merge step at the end to combine both, resolving conflicts by hand if git can't auto-merge. Sound good?

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implemented dry_run: pop dry_run from params, when True use io.BytesIO() as fp instead of opening/creating the real file, and skip fp.close()/file open when dry_run (guarded via 'open_fp and not dry_run'). Added docstring entry + 2 tests (test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_raises) right after test_string. Committing now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Implemented backup/backup_suffix params in Image.save() (popped right after save_all, backup-copy logic added in the open_fp block before file open using shutil.copy2). Added 3 tests (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_suffix, test_save_no_backup_by_default) right after test_string. All test_image.py tests pass (195 passed). Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: I fetched team/agent2 but it's still at the base commit (869aa5843) - your commit doesn't appear pushed yet. Could you run 'git push team agent2' (or your branch name) so I can merge it in for the final combined patch.txt?
